title: Replace transductive category stats with native time-aware CatBoost
summary: The current pipeline computes category codes/frequencies on train+test together and uses shuffled CatBoost defaults.
rationale: The current pipeline computes category codes/frequencies on train+test together and uses shuffled CatBoost defaults. CatBoost explicitly provides unbiased/ordered boosting plus `has_time` to avoid random permutations in categorical statistics, which is a closer match to sequential racing data and may remove subtle transductive leakage.
implementation_hint: Sort by Year/Race/LapNumber/id, pass raw categorical columns only, set `has_time=True`, feed a timestamp/order index, test CPU `boosting_type="Ordered"`, and tune `fold_permutation_block` around race or lap granularity. Compare against the current Cat model after removing any train+test frequency features from the XGB side.
expected_effect: Small but real lift from better handling of rare Driver/Race interactions and cleaner OOF estimates; also a safer foundation for future stacking.
risk: Ordered boosting on CPU is slower; gains may disappear if dataset size is already large enough that Plain GPU remains stronger.
